behaviors.description: A plugin with a manifest missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin executes.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at the handoff between manifest validation and module loading, not just a pure schema function. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: this is reproducible with in-process loader + real temp filesystem plugin package; no real network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System was considered but rejected because behavior text is about local plugin manifest rejection and execution gating, not OS/process/network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugin directory with an invalid `manifest.json` (missing required field) and an entry module with a visible side effect (e.g., writes marker file or flips shared flag on import). Run the real loader. Assert: validation error is reported for that plugin, marker/side effect never appears, and loader never calls that plugin's `onLoad`.

behaviors.description: If plugin A depends on plugin B, plugin B `onLoad` runs before plugin A `onLoad`.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when dependency resolution order is wired into lifecycle invocation across plugins. A unit test of topological sort alone misses hook-call integration. REAL-INFRA: local filesystem-backed plugin packages are sufficient; no subprocess/network. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text only concerns in-process dependency ordering and hook invocation.
behaviors.test_description: Create temp plugins A and B where A manifest depends on B. Each `onLoad` appends its name to shared in-memory/event log exposed by test harness. Run loader and assert call sequence is exactly `[B, A]`.

behaviors.description: Mutual dependency cycle is reported and neither plugin is loaded.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug boundary is between graph resolution and load execution suppression; requires modules interacting in loader flow. REAL-INFRA: can be caught with local temp plugin directories and real manifests in-process. No real OS/network/subprocess requirement beyond normal file reads. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text asks for cycle reporting and no-load policy, not external process/OS behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins A and B with manifests declaring dependencies on each other. Give each plugin entry/onLoad a detectable side effect. Run loader and assert: cycle error/report contains A<->B, and no side effects occurred for either plugin (entry import and `onLoad` both skipped).

behaviors.description: `onLoad` is invoked only after the plugin entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is sequencing across module import side effects and lifecycle hook invocation; unit-testing hook caller alone can miss it. REAL-INFRA: requires real module import execution semantics with local plugin files, but still in-process (not subprocess/system boundary). LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text is import/hook order inside Node loader flow, not external OS/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create a plugin entry module that sets `globalThis.pluginInitRan = true` at module top level, and exports `onLoad` that records value of that flag when called. Run loader and assert `onLoad` observed `true`, proving entry module executed before hook invocation.

behaviors.description: If a plugin throws during `onLoad`, loader continues with remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in orchestration of error handling + dependency-aware continuation across multiple plugins. Unit tests of try/catch logic alone are insufficient. REAL-INFRA: local plugin packages and in-process execution are enough; no real network/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text concerns loader control flow after hook exceptions, not external infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins: A (`onLoad` throws), B (independent), C (depends on A), and D (depends on B only). Run loader and assert: A failure is reported, B and D `onLoad` still run, C does not run due to dependency on failed A.
